title: Agarose gel pads for live Ashbya imaging
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bjqid","text":"A protocol to prepare agarose gel pads for live Ashbya imaging.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fouu2","text":"Rinse a depression slide with ethanol and have coverslips on hands.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4h80k","text":"Agarose is hot. Use gloves when handling the beaker and the tube.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"35unk","text":"2X LFM","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fnrk","text":"autoclaved dd H2O","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"529ue","text":"Overnight Ashbya culture","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"743lq","text":"Depression slide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4nvaq","text":"Agarose","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9e6vl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"68okk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f0eio","text":"Large beaker for water bath","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fmrno","text":"2 50 mL conicals","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"42l0q","text":"1 15 mL conical","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Make 20 mL 1X LFM stock before you start Always check LFM tube for contamination Reconstitute with ddH2O
Spin down overnight Ashbya culture to pellet cells in 15 mL conical tubes 00:05:00 300 RPM

Use a pipette to remove as much of the AFM as you can while leaving the cells at the bottom. Resuspend in 4 mL 1X LFM. Spin down again. 00:05:00 300 RPM. Either pipette pelleted cells from here onto the finished gel pad (steps 2.1-2.6), or do step 1.3 if the cells are too dense.
Optional: Resuspend in 1 mL 1X LFM. Incubate on the rotator at 30 °C until ready to make gel pad, or for 00:30:00 .
depression slide Depression slides live on Grace's desk. These are expensive, and we reuse them. Don't throw these out! Wash thoroughly with water and ethanol, dry them, and replace them in the box.
Aliquot 10 mL of 1X LFM from step 1 into a 50 mL conical.
Fill a 500 mL beaker with water to make a water bath and microwave the tube in 00:00:30 increments until the agarose is dissolved. Shake the tube to dissolve agarose.
Pipet 400 µL of the agarose solution into the depression on a depression slide.
Drag a coverslip across the depression to create the pad. After about 2-5 min, gently lift the coverslip off. The gel pad is now ready to use. steps 2.1-2.5
When ready, add 50 µL of the cells to the center of the pad. Gently cover it with a coverslip, and press gently with a kimwipe to squish the cells.

Stick a glass Pasteur pipette into a beaker of valap (you want to get the wax into the pipette). Then hold this over a Bunsen burner until the wax melts, and then apply around the edges of the coverslip to seal it: The slide is now ready to image.
creator: ameya.jalihal
